output: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) combines document retrieval with LLM generation to produce more accurate and grounded answers.
Fine-tuning is the process of training a pre-trained model on a specific dataset to adapt it to a task or domain.
LoRA is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that trains small adapter layers instead of updating the full model.
An LLM is a large language model trained on massive text data to generate human-like responses.
An embedding is a numerical vector representation of text used to measure semantic similarity.
A vector database stores embeddings and enables fast similarity search for RAG systems.
REST API is a web architecture style that uses HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE.
JSON is a lightweight data format used to exchange structured data between systems.
FastAPI is a Python framework for building fast and modern APIs.
Angular is a frontend framework for building single-page web applications.
Angular signals are reactive primitives used for managing state in Angular applications.
Dependency injection is a design pattern where dependencies are provided instead of created inside a class.
RxJS is a library for reactive programming using observables in JavaScript and Angular.

instruction: What is a component in Angular?
output: A component is a reusable UI block that includes HTML, CSS, and logic.
Angular routing allows navigation between different views in a single-page application.
A microservice is a small independent service that handles a specific function in a system architecture.
Docker is a platform for containerizing applications to ensure consistency across environments.
Kubernetes is a system for automating deployment and scaling of containerized applications.
CI/CD is a software development practice for continuous integration and continuous delivery.
Git is a version control system used to track code changes.
Hallucination occurs when an AI model generates incorrect or made-up information.
RAG reduces hallucination by grounding responses in retrieved documents instead of model memory.
Prompt engineering is the process of designing inputs to guide AI model outputs.
Context window is the maximum amount of text a model can process at once.
A token is a piece of text (word or subword) used by language models for processing.
Semantic search finds results based on meaning rather than exact keyword matching.
Similarity search finds items closest to a query based on vector distance.
Cosine similarity measures the angle between two vectors to determine similarity.
FAISS is a library for efficient similarity search of embeddings.
Chunking is splitting documents into smaller parts for better retrieval in RAG systems.
Temperature controls randomness in model outputs; higher values produce more creative responses.
Top-p sampling selects tokens based on cumulative probability to control randomness.
Zero-shot learning is when a model performs a task without specific training examples.
Few-shot learning uses a small number of examples to guide model output.
Instruction tuning trains models to follow natural language instructions.
Overfitting happens when a model learns training data too well and fails to generalize.
Underfitting happens when a model fails to learn patterns from data.
Supervised learning trains models using labeled input-output pairs.
Unsupervised learning finds patterns in data without labeled outputs.
Reinforcement learning trains models using rewards and penalties based on actions.
Latency is the time delay between a request and a response in a system.
Scalability is the ability of a system to handle increased load efficiently.
Caching stores frequently used data for faster access.
Authentication is the process of verifying a user's identity.
Authorization determines what actions a user is allowed to perform.
